codeReview.summary: I reviewed the prior failing review, the round-1 synthesis, the latest worker handoff/transcript skeleton, and the fix diff in commit `e9d67b0`. The three prior blocking `lib/codegen.ml` branches are now gone: imported call return inference uses `Interop.fn_type`, imported field inference uses `Interop.receiver_field`, and imported receiver-method inference uses `Interop.receiver_method`, with `interop_ty_to_ast_ty` bridging registry `Types.ty` values back into codegen inference. The current `Interop` registry still defines `http::new_serve_mux`, `Request.method`, and `Request.form_value`, so the affected paths remain correct while codegen no longer hardcodes those `net/http` cases.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `compiler-worker` skill's tests-first rule is still ambiguous for narrow follow-up fixes that intentionally rely on existing coverage. This re-run reported `followedProcedure: true` while adding no tests and explicitly relying on pre-existing suites plus a manual `.rg -> .go` check.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/compiler-worker/SKILL.md:22 requires workers to 'Write tests first' and add a real `.rg -> .go` acceptance check for codegen-affecting interop work, while SKILL.md:28 says tests-first deviations should set `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` to `false`. summary: Pass. The fix in `e9d67b0` resolves the three prior blocking `lib/codegen.ml` issues by routing `http::new_serve_mux`, `Request.method`, and `form_value` inference through shared interop metadata, and I did not find any new feature-scoped correctness issues.
